items.title: Global population at 8.3 billion: What does that mean for anyone under 30?
items.description: With the world population surpassing 8.3 billion today, can we truly realize the hopes and dreams of the largest generation of young people ever? On World Population Day 2026, the spotlight is on empowering youth to shape their futures through education, health, and opportunity. From family planning to sustainable development, this global observance asks a vital question: are we investing enough in tomorrow's leaders today? Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Diene Keita, Executive Director of the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).

items.title: The Rise and Pause of Jordan Bardella
items.description: The political landscape in France changed this week after the appeal court judges upheld the guilty verdict against Marine Le Pen for misappropriating European Parliament funds, but reduced the five-year ban on her standing for public office to fifteen months. Time already served, said the Paris court. With Marine Le Pen no longer the presidential hopeful for the single most popular party in France right now, the far-right National Rally.

items.title: U.S. Democrat Ro Khanna detained briefly by Israeli settlers in Palestine
items.description: Mr. Khanna, a Democrat from California, was visiting the ruins of Khirbet Zanuta, a Bedouin village in the southern West Bank that was abandoned after attacks from Israeli settlers when the incident took place

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: July 3 to 10
items.description: A selection of the stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Could AI help al-Qaeda and other groups plan terror attacks?
items.description: Followers of extremist groups regularly ask how AI can help them plan terrorist attacks. A new study suggests that about one-third of AI chatbots might help them, if asked the right way.

items.title: Malian army retakes key northern town of Anefis after week-long battle
items.description: Mali's army has regained control of the key northern town of Anefis after fighting against rebel militants for nearly a week, Tuareg separatists and a military source said Friday.

items.title: 11 wounded in overnight Russian missile and drone strikes on Kyiv
items.description: At least 11 people, including a child, were wounded after Russia launched an overnight missile and drone attack on Kyiv early Saturday, according to Ukraine’s State Emergency Service.

items.title: US Democrat Ro Khanna says he was detained by armed Israeli settlers
items.description: Khanna, 49, was visiting the occupied West Bank when he was held for 90 minutes, he says.

items.title: IMF confirms 3.5% growth forecast for Argentina in 2026
items.description: Multilateral lender’s chief Kristalina Georgieva to visit Buenos Aires this month; International Monetary Fund sees Argentina’s economy expanding 4% in 2027. Leer más

items.title: At least 88 terrorists killed in ongoing anti-terror operation in Pakistan’s Balochistan
items.description: The military, paramilitary Rangers and the Frontier Corps are participating in the ongoing operation, which has involved both ground and air offensives against the militants

items.title: NATO | Towards a more European alliance
items.description: Faced with external security challenges and internal divisions, especially under a sceptical Donald Trump administration, European members are preparing to play a larger role in keeping the alliance united and functional

items.title: Watch: Inside the 30-hour dating retreat run by monks
items.description: Forget coffee or drinks, the latest dating trend in South Korea takes place somewhere very different, a Buddhist temple.

items.title: World Cup: Bellingham’s brace fires England into semi-finals as Haaland crashes out with Norway
items.description: England will now await the winners of the quarter-final between Argentina and Switzerland as they continue their pursuit of a place in the World Cup final The post World Cup: Bellingham’s brace fires England into semi-finals as Haaland crashes out with Norway appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Third US strike on Iran triggers strait closure, attacks on regional bases
items.description: Iran reportedly responded to Sunday morning's US attacks by closing the Strait of Hormuz and targeting US bases in Qatar, Kuwait and Jordan.

items.title: China evacuates over 1 million as Typhoon Bavi brings winds and rain
items.description: Authorities in Zhejiang province have evacuated over 1.7 million people, while Shanghai relocated about 34,000 residents from high-risk areas by Saturday noon

items.title: 'Beat the bastard': Arrow vows to help others fight MND
items.description: Former NRL player Jai Arrow says he is determined to raise money and awareness for Motor Neurone Disease until a cure is found.

items.title: Central Bank survey sees inflation dipping below 2% from August
items.description: REM market expectations survey of consultancy firms, economists and experts forecasts 2026 annual inflation of 30%, with peso trading at 1,673 per US dollar by year-end. Leer más

items.title: Boat carrying tourists had barely left shore when it capsized, says witness
items.description: Weather conditions were not adverse, says Indian national

items.title: What happens when your fun-loving grandfather — a good husband, an upright citizen — turns out to have been a Nazi?
items.description: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

items.title: Argentina to repay US$4 billion, defying critics who doubted tack
items.description: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible. Leer más

items.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
items.description: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

items.title: U.S. military says it is striking Iran in response to attack on civilian vessel in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The United States is imposing a heavy cost by continuing to degrade Iran's ability to attack civilian mariners and commercial ships freely transiting the strait, the American military said.

items.title: Tour de France stage to be shortened due to 'intense heatwave'
items.description: Tour de France's organisers on Saturday announced that they will shorten a stage of the race due to intense heat. ASO made this historic decision to cut out a hilly loop at the start of the stageafter France's weather authority declared a red alert in Corrèze.

items.title: Nigeria’s eroding Orimedu coast threatens homes and livelihoods
items.description: This coastline along Orimedu in Nigeria is facing land degradation. The people here are seeing the destruction of their homes as well as the loss of livelihoods.

items.title: Trump says US will 'completely decimate' Iran if assassinated
items.description: President Donald Trump said Friday the United States would "completely decimate" Iran if it attempted or succeeded in assassinating the sitting president.

items.title: When London stank to high heaven
items.description: In 1858, London sweltered through an unbearable heat wave. The River Thames reeked so badly it left people gasping for air. "The Great Stink" went down in history — and ultimately gave London a modern sewer system.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: 2 dead and 3 wounded in shooting near Toronto street festival, police say
items.description: Two people have been killed and three others wounded in a shooting near a Toronto street festival, prompting police to warn of an active shooter before later saying the scene had been secured

items.title: Migrant backlash risks South Africa’s economy
items.description: Economists warn that if thousands of foreign workers leave, it could damage the very businesses and jobs protesters say they want to protect.

items.title: US pays out $3m to victims of mystery Havana Syndrome condition reported by spies
items.description: US officials and diplomats and their families began reporting a mysterious illness a decade ago.

items.title: Lionel Scaloni: 'I became a coach so I could experience these emotions'
items.description: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni hails his side's character after Tuesday's dramatic 3-2 comeback victory over Egypt. Leer más

items.title: Iran bids farewell to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei amid tensions with US
items.description: Large crowds of mourners marched through Mashhad on 9 July for the burial of former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at Mashhad’s Imam Reza Shrine.

items.title: A veteran foreign correspondent looks back on a career covering conflicts
items.description: For Reporter's Notebook we hear about what it takes to cover conflict over a decades-long career as a foreign correspondent.

items.title: The photo that shows who's really on top in Le Pen's party
items.description: Thirty-year-old Jordan Bardella had been groomed for success as France's next hard-right leader. He will now have to wait longer.

items.title: Deadly fire at China shoe factory sparks Xi Jinping order for full probe
items.description: China is investigating a fire that killed 28 people at a shoe factory in Jinjiang, Fujian Province, on 9 July.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI, two former employees for trade secrets theft
California’s largest lake is turning to dust
items.description: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for people living near its shores. Authorities are working to stabilize the lakebed and lock down the dust. But for some families, it's too late.

items.title: Noskova survives tearful meltdown to win first Wimbledon title
items.description: Linda Noskova won her first Wimbledon title on Saturday in one of the most dramatic finals in All England Club history. The 21-year-old recovered from a second-set meltdown to overcome Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3. Noskova is the third Czech woman in four years to win the grass-court major, after Marketa Vondrousova in 2023 and Barbora Krejcikova in 2024.

items.title: China evacuates nearly two million people as powerful typhoon makes landfall
items.description: Schools, work and outdoor activities have been suspended in Zhejiang province and many transport services cancelled.

items.title: US and its Latin American partners urge 'peaceful' transition in Colombia
items.description: In addition to Washington, signatories included Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guyana, Honduras, Panama, Paraguay, and Trinidad and Tobago. Leer más
